title: Quest for the Lost Pixel - Play Through and Legacy Variant Discussion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Cy2qJ0Win1I

Daniel (@dungeondive) rolls up his sleeves for a proper playthrough of Quest for the Lost Pixel—not merely explaining rules, but demonstrating actual dungeon delving while simultaneously workshopping a **legacy variant** that transforms this roguelike into something approaching Rogue Legacy's meta-progression.

**The Legacy Concept**: Daniel's experimenting with an exploration currency earned from engaging with dungeon features. These tokens accumulate across runs, eventually purchasing village buildings or preserving items for future adventurers. Death needn't mean starting from absolute zero—your fallen heroes' discoveries benefit their successors.

**The Trophy System**: Twelve proposed achievements providing long-term campaign goals: complete a treasure set, catch a trophy fish, find five legendary treasures, win without using gems, defeat three bosses, complete the Catacombs, betray your god, keep your pet alive through victory. Complete all twelve, and you've truly conquered Lost Pixel.

**The Playthrough**: Daniel draws the Paladin (naturally), pairs him with Horus the pig companion, worships Garus the god of balance (no attacking bosses!), and descends. The demonstration covers movement, events, monster combat, elemental damage, equipment management, spell usage, gem expenditure, and—in a clutch final turn—an Astral Phase event that warps the Paladin directly to the previously impossible exit.

"Never give up. You never know what's going to happen in Quest for the Lost Pixel."

The Catacombs receive special attention, with Daniel walking through the press-your-luck mechanics: ten energy tokens, cards costing one to three energy each, the agonising decision whether to press deeper or retreat with current gains. Bust by exceeding your energy, and you leave empty-handed.

**Viewer Caveat**: Daniel acknowledges mistakes are inevitable when filming and playing simultaneously. For rules questions, he recommends BGG or contacting Pete Jank directly.

---

*Have you ever house-ruled a favourite game into something substantially different, and did the surgery succeed?*
